note: 8 of 9 phase changes are archived: p16-c001 through p16-c007, and p16-c009 (Round 4, VGV quality gates — coverage gate, deny.toml, #![deny(missing_docs)] on all 22 library crates with # Errors sections, dependency dedup 160->149, unsafe-block audit, error-swallow review that found and fixed two real bugs). Only p16-c008 (production operations) remains open. Its scope changed significantly after the GitHub Environments were created (with the user's explicit confirmation) and a later concurrent session reworked the production deploy model from SSH/compose to GitOps (ArgoCD Application + AKS, Azure Workload Identity) — this superseded the SSH-key and static-S3-credential provisioning items entirely (Workload Identity needs no static secrets), and both the restore drill and the k6 performance run are now self-triggering CronJobs/CI jobs rather than manual operator actions. The one genuinely remaining item: none of that automation has executed yet, because flint-forge has not actually been deployed to a real cluster. An operator must apply the ArgoCD Application (or `helm install`) to a real AKS cluster, let the restore-drill CronJob run at least once against real backup data, and record the result in docs/runbook.md §13.5 — this cannot be done from this environment (no cloud credentials, no running cluster).
